title: Work-order dispatch dogfood — the loop cannot run a real work-order today; five breaks root-caused, one architectural fact seen from three sides
question: When a real work-order is filed and driven through run-work-order-loop from pi -p non-interactive dispatch, where does the machinery break, what is the root cause of each break, and is the work-order loop usable as a harness dispatch primitive today?
method: User-directed live probe (WO-001: a minimal real work-order whose agent must write a file, gated by a deterministic runtime-demo real-check) driven from pi -p; fresh-context investigation root-causing each break against current source with an instrumented completeFn reproduction against built dist; git-date archaeology; substrate prior-art sweep via the CLI.
findings_summary: The loop runs end to end without crashing but cannot execute any real work-order. Highest-value root cause (empirically confirmed): pi-jit-agents executeAgent is a single-turn completion primitive whose context.tools is populated only by a phantom output-schema tool — a dispatched agent granted ['write','bash'] receives ZERO executable tools (instrumented: toolsPassedToLLM UNDEFINED), so the demo agent's 'cannot write files' was accurate and every honest work-order must fail at iteration 0. Breaks 2, 3, 5 are one architectural fact from three sides: the work-orders schema (2026-05-27) is a v1 contract — typed input, context injection, output validation, operation-scoped clamp, bounded retry — whose loop (2026-05-28) implements a demo slice on a primitive that cannot act; the schema even asserts present-tense engine behavior (scope 'Used by the capability composer to clamp') that does not run, and ships in the installable catalog. Breaks 1 and 4 compound at the boundary: the sanctioned agent-authoring tool is interactively gated (no non-interactive path for the 8 pi-only gated tools), and no ceremony seeds the <contextDir>/agents/ tier the loader resolves from — so on a fresh substrate driven from pi -p the loop's precondition is unproducible. Only the deterministic real-check behaved correctly, which is why this surfaced as a clean failure not a false pass. The dispatch-architecture choice (pi-subprocess vs executeAgent tool-loop vs output-only re-scope) is a decision the fix must make first. Also flagged: loop/tool doc comments cite frozen .project-era IDs that collide with unrelated active-.context items.

revisions: workflowsPiExtension main @ 3b84e895 (2026-07-07); dispatch code re-read from source at investigation time

stale_conditions: Any change to pi-jit-agents executeAgent tool-binding (jit-runtime.ts) or the work-order loop's field consumption (work-order-loop.ts)

citations.label: executeAgent single-turn primitive; phantom-tool-only context.tools; grant clamp
loop consumes 5 of 9 required fields; clamp on tool param not wo.scope.operations
auth gate unconditional hasUI=false refusal; gated-tool set
findings document: five-break root-cause, instrumented reproduction, git-date archaeology, findings table
